theme: Whisperer panel UI in the rendered frame, monster/item naming on first sight, atmospheric room prose
summary: Sprint 7 delivered the Whisperer plumbing (event bus, LLM adapters, fallback prose pool, budget guardrails, deterministic offline whispers, Game wiring for run_started + descended). Sprint 8 makes the Whisperer VISIBLE: it adds an in-frame whisper panel to the rendered output so players actually read what the Whisperer is saying. It also introduces two semantic event sources that wire into the existing bus: (1) first-sight naming — the first time the player encounters a particular monster KIND or item KIND, the Whisperer mints a short evocative name and that name is reused for the rest of the run; and (2) atmospheric room prose — when the player enters a previously-unseen room, the Whisperer emits a 1-2 sentence room description. The dungeon glyph grid itself is unchanged; the panel sits beside or below the grid and is composited into the frame string by the renderer. Sprint 8 is still text-only; no curses TUI, no color codes, no input changes. Determinism, layering, and the no-network test posture established in Sprint 7 are preserved. Items and monsters do not need a real Sprint-4/5 implementation in tree to satisfy this sprint: a minimal kind-registry sufficient for naming-on-sight is added in this sprint, and tests publish first-sight events to the bus directly where higher-fidelity world hooks are not yet present.
assumptions: Sprint 7 is merged in tree: whisperdeep/events.py, whisperdeep/llm.py, whisperdeep/whisperer.py, whisperdeep/adapter_factory.py, whisperdeep/game.py with whisperer wiring, whisperdeep/cli.py with --whisperer/--no-whisperer/--dump-whispers, and the prose_pool.json fallback resource all exist and pass their Sprint-7 evaluation. handoff/eval-report-07 confirm 22/22 Sprint-7 criteria PASS and 66/66 pytest green with no API keys.

Sprints 3, 4, 5, 6 are NOT merged in tree (handoff lists completedSprints=[1,2]; only Sprint 7 was retro-applied for plumbing). Sprint 8 must therefore NOT depend on Sprint-4 inventory or Sprint-5 monster spawn machinery existing. Where naming-on-sight needs a 'kind' to name, Sprint 8 adds a minimal kind registry (a small JSON or Python data file plus a tiny first_sight publisher) and wires it through the existing event bus. Tests publish first-sight events directly to the bus where higher-fidelity hooks do not yet exist.

The renderer (whisperdeep/render.py) currently produces a multiline string of the dungeon grid with the player overlaid. Sprint 8 extends rendering by ADDING a panel composition layer on top of render_frame (e.g., render_frame_with_whispers, or an option flag) WITHOUT changing the underlying dungeon glyph rows for the same seed. This protects the Sprint-2 byte-level seed determinism contract.

Whisper panel is text only. No color codes, no curses, no TUI library. The panel is plain ASCII with optional box-drawing characters (e.g., '|' or '│' on the left edge). Lines are wrapped to a documented panel width (e.g., 30 columns) so output remains stable across terminals.

First-sight naming is per-RUN, not cross-run. The Whisperer maintains an in-memory map of {kind_id: name} for the duration of a Game; the same kind seen twice in one run uses the same name; a new run mints fresh names. Cross-run persistence of names is Sprint 9/10 territory and is OUT OF SCOPE.
Atmospheric room prose is keyed by (floor_index, room_id) and emitted at most once per room per run. Re-entering a previously-described room does NOT trigger a new whisper.

All new event types added in this sprint (e.g., first_sight, room_entered) extend the canonical EVENT_TYPES set from Sprint 7 in a backward-compatible way: existing seven names remain present, the new names are appended. The OfflineAdapter pool gains >= 8 entries for each newly-canonical event type so determinism and fallback work without a real LLM.

Determinism: with --seed N --headless and the offline adapter, the rendered frame (panel + grid) is byte-identical across separate processes for the same seed. The first-sight naming and the room-prose ordering are reproducible from the seed alone.

Pytest remains the test runner; tests live under tests/. Tests must NOT make network calls. The Sprint-7 layering invariants (Game -> bus only; Whisperer -> bus + adapter-ABC; concrete adapters via factory) are preserved.

criteria.description: A whisper-panel rendering surface exists and is importable. Either (a) whisperdeep/render.py exposes a new public function like `render_panel(whispers: Iterable[Whisper], *, width: int = 30, height: int = 12) -> str` that returns a multiline string of fixed dimensions, OR (b) a new module like `whisperdeep/panel.py` exposes the equivalent. The function takes Whisper-like records and returns a deterministic ASCII block.
testSteps: Run `python -c "from whisperdeep.render import render_panel"` OR `python -c "from whisperdeep.panel import render_panel"` and confirm exit code 0.
Inspect the function signature: it MUST accept an iterable/list of Whisper records (or dicts with at least a 'text' key) and return a str.
Verify the function accepts a `width` (or equivalent) keyword argument with a documented default.
criteria.threshold: render_panel (or named equivalent) is importable, takes whispers, and returns a string.

criteria.description: The whisper panel renders the most recent N whispers (newest at the bottom or newest at the top — either is acceptable as long as it is documented and consistent), wraps each whisper to the panel width, prefixes/visually separates whispers from each other (e.g., a leading marker like `> ` or a blank-line separator or a box-drawing rule), and pads the panel to a fixed width and height so the dungeon frame next to it is not jittered.
testSteps: Construct a list of 5 Whisper records with texts of varying lengths (a 5-word, a 30-word, and three 10-word texts).
Call render_panel(whispers, width=30, height=12) (or the documented signature).
Split the result on '\n'; assert every line has length == 30 (panel is left-padded or right-padded to exactly the panel width).
Assert the number of lines == 12 (panel is exactly height tall, even if whispers are fewer).
Verify the long 30-word whisper is wrapped onto multiple lines AND no line exceeds width=30.
Verify each whisper's text content (after wrapping and separator removal) appears at least once in the panel string.
Verify two adjacent whispers are visually distinguishable (a separator marker, blank line, or per-whisper prefix is present).
criteria.threshold: Panel is fixed-width rectangle; wraps long whispers; visually separates entries; pads to fixed height.

criteria.description: When the panel must show more whispers than fit, OLDER whispers are dropped from the visible window (a sliding window). The whisper log itself (game.whisperer.whispers) is NOT mutated — only the rendered view is windowed.
testSteps: Build a list of 50 short whispers labeled 'w0' .. 'w49'.
Call render_panel(whispers, width=30, height=12) (or the documented signature) and capture the output string.
Assert the most-recent whisper text 'w49' appears in the panel.
Assert the oldest whisper text 'w0' does NOT appear in the panel.
Assert the visible whisper texts are contiguous from some index k to 49 (no gaps).
Verify the original list of 50 whispers is unchanged (length still 50, identity of element 0 still 'w0').
criteria.threshold: Panel windows the most-recent whispers; original whisper log is not mutated.

criteria.description: A composite frame renderer composes the dungeon grid AND the whisper panel into a single multiline string for headless display. Either (a) `render_frame_with_whispers(game)` (or named equivalent) is exposed in whisperdeep/render.py, OR (b) `render_frame(game, *, with_panel=True)` accepts an opt-in flag. The composite places the panel either to the right of the grid (each line has `<grid_row>  <panel_row>`) or directly below the grid (panel rows after a blank line). The choice is documented; tests verify the documented choice.
testSteps: Construct `Game.from_seed(seed=1, whisperer=True)` (Sprint 7 wiring puts at least a 'run_started' whisper into the log).
Call the composite renderer (the documented function or flag).
Split the output on '\n'.
Assert the dungeon glyph rows (those containing '#', '.', or '@') are all present, in order, and unchanged from `render_frame(game)`'s glyph content for the same seed.
Assert the panel content (at least one whisper text from `game.whisperer.whispers`) appears in the composite output.

Verify the composite layout matches the documented choice (right-of-grid OR below-grid) — e.g., if right-of-grid, every line that contains a glyph also contains the panel separator or panel content; if below-grid, the panel block follows the grid block.
criteria.threshold: Composite renderer combines grid + panel; documented layout; whisper text is visible; grid content unchanged.

criteria.description: The CLI default headless run shows the whisper panel. Running `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless` prints the composite frame (grid + panel) to stdout and includes at least one whisper text drawn from the offline pool (e.g., the run_started whisper). Running `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer` prints ONLY the dungeon grid (no panel, no whisper banner) — byte-equivalent to the Sprint-2 frame for the same seed.
testSteps: Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless` and capture stdout as composite_out (env: API keys cleared).
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer` and capture stdout as grid_only_out.
Assert composite_out exit code 0 and grid_only_out exit code 0.

Assert grid_only_out contains the dungeon glyphs ('#', '.', '@') and contains NO panel separator marker (no '|' panel-edge character if that is the chosen marker; no '>' whisper-prefix lines that don't correspond to downstairs).
Assert composite_out contains all the dungeon glyph rows that grid_only_out contains (the grid is preserved; no glyphs were repurposed).

Assert composite_out contains at least one substring drawn from the offline prose pool (i.e., a real whisper). Hint: load whisperdeep/prose_pool.json's 'run_started' entries and assert at least one of them appears verbatim in composite_out.
criteria.threshold: Default --headless prints grid + panel with at least one real whisper; --no-whisperer prints grid only; both exit 0.

criteria.description: Sprint-7's plumbing-only invariant is preserved at the API level: the dungeon glyph grid produced by `render_frame(game)` (the original Sprint-2 function) is byte-identical to what it produced before Sprint 8 for the same seed. Sprint 8 must NOT modify the grid string; it must only ADD a panel layer in a NEW function or via an opt-in flag.
testSteps: Construct `Game.from_seed(seed=1, whisperer=True)` and capture A = render_frame(game) (Sprint-2 signature, default arguments).
Construct `Game.from_seed(seed=1, whisperer=False)` and capture B = render_frame(game).
Assert A == B byte-for-byte (the whisperer being on or off must NOT affect the grid produced by `render_frame`).
Verify A contains '#', '.', '@' (Sprint-1/2 glyph contract).
If `render_frame` accepts a `with_panel` flag, also call `render_frame(game, with_panel=False)` and assert it equals A; only `with_panel=True` should produce the composite.
criteria.threshold: Original render_frame(game) is byte-identical with whisperer on vs off; no grid mutation.

criteria.description: First-sight monster/item naming: a `first_sight` event type is added to the canonical EVENT_TYPES set. When fired with a payload like {'kind': 'goblin', 'category': 'monster'} (or 'item'), the Whisperer mints a name (a string drawn from the offline pool or LLM) and stores it. Re-firing first_sight for the SAME kind on the same run does NOT mint a new name — the existing name is reused (idempotent). The name registry is exposed (e.g., `game.whisperer.names` or `whisperer.get_name(kind)`).
testSteps: Import EVENT_TYPES (or EventType enum) from whisperdeep.events; assert 'first_sight' is present.
Construct an EventBus and a Whisperer with OfflineAdapter(seed=1).
Publish an Event(type='first_sight', payload={'kind': 'goblin', 'category': 'monster'}, turn=1, floor=0).
Assert the whisperer produced a whisper whose text is non-empty.
Read the name registry (`whisperer.names['goblin']` OR `whisperer.get_name('goblin')`) and capture name1.
Publish a SECOND Event(type='first_sight', payload={'kind': 'goblin', 'category': 'monster'}, turn=2, floor=0).
Assert the registered name is still name1 (no second mint), even though a new whisper may or may not be produced.

Publish first_sight for kind='ghoul'; assert a second name (name2) is registered, name2 != name1 (or, if the pool only has one entry, document and accept identical strings — but the pool MUST be large enough to make collisions unlikely).
criteria.threshold: first_sight is canonical; same kind -> same name; different kinds -> different names; idempotent.

criteria.description: Atmospheric room-entered prose: a `room_entered` event type is added to the canonical EVENT_TYPES set. When fired with a payload like {'floor': i, 'room_id': r}, the Whisperer emits a 1-2 sentence whisper. Re-entering the SAME (floor, room_id) does NOT produce a second whisper for that room (per-room dedupe within a run).
testSteps: Assert 'room_entered' is in EVENT_TYPES (or EventType enum).
Construct an EventBus and a Whisperer with OfflineAdapter(seed=2).
Publish an Event(type='room_entered', payload={'floor': 0, 'room_id': 0}, turn=1, floor=0).
Assert one new whisper appeared whose source_event_type == 'room_entered'.
Publish a SECOND Event(type='room_entered', payload={'floor': 0, 'room_id': 0}, turn=2, floor=0) (same room).
Assert NO new whisper was produced for that room (whisper count unchanged for source_event_type == 'room_entered').
Publish Event(type='room_entered', payload={'floor': 0, 'room_id': 1}, turn=3, floor=0) (different room).
Assert ONE more room_entered whisper appears (count went from 1 -> 2).
Publish Event(type='room_entered', payload={'floor': 1, 'room_id': 0}, turn=4, floor=1) (same room_id, different floor).
Assert ONE more room_entered whisper appears (count 2 -> 3): dedupe is keyed on the (floor, room_id) pair.
criteria.threshold: room_entered is canonical; per-(floor, room_id) dedupe within a run; new rooms always produce a whisper.

criteria.description: The OfflineAdapter prose pool is extended to cover the new event types: at least 8 distinct entries each for 'first_sight' and 'room_entered'. Total distinct entries across all canonical event types is now >= 72 (Sprint 7 had >= 56; Sprint 8 adds >= 16). The Sprint-7 entries are preserved unchanged (no regression on the seven original types).
testSteps: Locate the prose-pool resource (whisperdeep/prose_pool.json or equivalent).
Load it and assert pool['first_sight'] has >= 8 distinct non-empty string entries.
Assert pool['room_entered'] has >= 8 distinct non-empty string entries.
Assert each of the seven original Sprint-7 event types still has >= 8 distinct entries (no regression).
Assert the total count of distinct entries across all canonical event types is >= 72.
criteria.threshold: >= 8 entries per new event type; >= 72 total; Sprint-7 pool not regressed.

criteria.description: First-sight whispers SUBSTITUTE the minted name into the prose. A first_sight pool entry contains a placeholder (e.g., `{name}` or `${name}` or a sentinel string) that is replaced with the freshly minted name in the produced whisper text. Tests verify that the rendered whisper contains the registered name string, not the raw placeholder.
testSteps: Inspect a sample first_sight pool entry (or document the templating convention in code/docs).
Construct a Whisperer with OfflineAdapter(seed=3).
Publish first_sight for kind='goblin'.
Read the produced whisper.text and the registered name `whisperer.get_name('goblin')`.
Assert whisper.text CONTAINS the registered name string verbatim.
Assert whisper.text does NOT contain a literal placeholder substring (e.g., '{name}' or '${name}') — substitution actually happened.
Repeat for kind='healing potion' (category='item') and verify the same substitution behavior.
criteria.threshold: first_sight whispers contain the minted name and not the raw template placeholder.

criteria.description: The Game publishes room_entered events when the player enters a new room. Either (a) Game.try_move detects room transitions and publishes room_entered, OR (b) a helper hook is added that is called by the rendering loop / explicit transition logic. At minimum, on Game.from_seed (whisperer=True), the room containing the spawn point produces a room_entered event for floor 0, and Game.descend produces a room_entered event for the room containing the upstairs landing on the new floor.
testSteps: Construct `Game.from_seed(seed=1, whisperer=True)`.
Inspect game.whisperer.whispers; assert at least one whisper has source_event_type == 'room_entered' for floor 0 (the spawn room).
Place the player on the downstairs of floor 0 (use game.teleport(...) with the floor's downstairs_pos).
Call game.descend(); assert a 'descended' whisper appears (Sprint 7 contract preserved).
Assert ALSO a 'room_entered' whisper appears for floor 1's landing room (source_floor == 1).
Move the player out of the landing room and into a different room on the same floor (use teleport for test simplicity; the sprint may also implement room-detection on movement).
Assert another 'room_entered' whisper appears for the new room.
Move the player BACK to the landing room.
Assert NO new room_entered whisper is produced (per-(floor, room_id) dedupe from C8 holds in the integration path).
criteria.threshold: Game lifecycle publishes room_entered for spawn room and on descent; movement-driven room transitions publish room_entered with dedupe honored.

criteria.description: First-sight integration: a documented hook on the Game (e.g., `game.observe_kind(kind, category)` OR automatic publication when an entity is rendered for the first time) publishes a `first_sight` event for any new kind. Calling the hook twice for the same kind is idempotent (no duplicate first_sight, no second name mint). Even without a real Sprint-5 monster spawn system, the hook is callable directly by tests and by future sprints.
testSteps: Construct `Game.from_seed(seed=1, whisperer=True)`.
Call `game.observe_kind('skitterer', category='monster')` (or the documented Sprint-8 hook).
Assert one new first_sight whisper appeared and `game.whisperer.get_name('skitterer')` returns a non-empty string.
Call `game.observe_kind('skitterer', category='monster')` again.
Assert NO new first_sight whisper was produced (idempotent at the Game layer).
Call `game.observe_kind('rust ring', category='item')`.
Assert one more first_sight whisper appears and `game.whisperer.get_name('rust ring')` is registered.
Verify the hook does NOT raise when called with `whisperer=False` Game (it is a no-op or a documented safe error).
criteria.threshold: Game.observe_kind (or named equivalent) exists, integrates with first_sight, is idempotent, and is safe with whisperer disabled.

criteria.description: End-to-end whisper-and-frame determinism: with `--seed N --headless`, the FULL stdout (banner + composite frame, including the panel content and minted names) is byte-identical across two separate processes for the same seed. Different seeds produce a stdout that differs in at least one whisper or one minted name.
testSteps: Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 11 --headless` twice in separate processes; capture stdout as out_A and out_B.
Assert out_A == out_B byte-for-byte.
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 12 --headless`; capture as out_C.
Assert out_A != out_C (some whisper text or minted name differs because the seed differs).

Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 11 --headless --dump-whispers w11.json` twice; load both files and assert their JSON arrays are element-wise equal (extending the Sprint-7 dump determinism contract).
Confirm each dumped whisper has source_event_type that is one of the canonical types including the new 'first_sight' / 'room_entered' if those fired during the run.
criteria.threshold: Same seed -> identical stdout AND identical whisper dump; different seeds -> different stdout.

criteria.description: CLI flags exist to control the panel. At minimum: a `--no-panel` (or `--panel=off`) flag suppresses the whisper panel without disabling the Whisperer (so whispers still accumulate in the log and `--dump-whispers` still works); a `--panel-width N` (or equivalent) sets the panel width. The default behavior shows the panel. All new flags appear in `--help`.
testSteps: Run `python -m whisperdeep --help` and verify the help text mentions the new panel flag(s) (e.g., '--no-panel', '--panel-width').
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-panel` and capture stdout as no_panel_out; assert exit code 0.
Assert no_panel_out does NOT contain prose-pool entries (the panel is suppressed) but still exits cleanly.
Assert running with --no-panel and --dump-whispers WHISPER_FILE writes a non-empty whisper array to WHISPER_FILE (whispers still produced even though hidden).

Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --panel-width 20`; assert exit 0 and that any panel line in stdout has length <= 20 characters (modulo the grid columns; if right-of-grid layout, parse panel columns specifically).
Verify `--no-whisperer` continues to suppress both panel and whispers (Sprint-7 behavior preserved).
criteria.threshold: --no-panel and --panel-width work; both are documented in --help; --no-whisperer remains the strongest off switch.

criteria.description: The whisper panel never crashes the renderer. Edge cases must be handled gracefully: zero whispers (panel renders an empty-but-correctly-sized block), one whisper longer than 5x panel width (wraps onto multiple lines, never raises), unicode whispers (handled without UnicodeEncodeError on stdout), and a panel height of 1 (only the most recent whisper's first line shows).
testSteps: Call render_panel([], width=30, height=12); assert returns a string of 12 lines each of width 30 (blank but correctly sized).
Build a Whisper whose text is 'word ' * 200 (a 1000-character string); call render_panel([w], width=30, height=12).
Assert NO exception is raised, every line is exactly 30 chars, and the visible content is the FIRST chunk that fits in 12 lines.
Build a Whisper with non-ASCII text (e.g., 'the floor remembers — the dark exhales'); call render_panel([w], width=30, height=4).
Assert NO UnicodeError is raised and the dash character appears in the output.
Call render_panel([w1, w2, w3], width=30, height=1); assert exactly 1 line of length 30 is returned.
Verify render_panel does NOT mutate the input list (length and identity unchanged after the call).
criteria.threshold: Panel handles 0/1/many whispers, long text, unicode, and tiny dimensions without raising; never mutates inputs.

criteria.description: Whisper categories are visually distinguishable in the panel: at minimum, an atmospheric room_entered whisper and a first_sight whisper are presented with a per-category prefix or marker (e.g., room_entered prefix `~`, first_sight prefix `*`, generic prefix `>`). The convention is documented in code/docs and stable. Tests verify the marker is present for each category in the rendered panel string.
testSteps: Construct three Whisper records: one with source_event_type='room_entered', one with source_event_type='first_sight', one with source_event_type='killed_monster'.
Call render_panel([w_room, w_sight, w_kill], width=40, height=12).
Assert the rendered panel contains the documented marker for each category at least once.
Verify the markers for the three categories are NOT all identical (they are distinguishable from each other).
criteria.threshold: Per-category prefix/marker is rendered and documented; categories are visually distinguishable.

criteria.description: Budget guardrail and per-turn throttle from Sprint 7 are still respected when the new event types fire. Publishing 50 first_sight events for distinct kinds within one turn does NOT bypass the per-turn cap; the Whisperer caps per-turn whispers at the documented small number, just as it does for the original seven types.
testSteps: Construct a CountingAdapter that records call_count.
Construct a Whisperer with the CountingAdapter, per_turn_cap=3 (Sprint-7 default).
Publish 50 first_sight events for distinct kinds k0..k49, all on turn=1.
Assert CountingAdapter.call_count <= 3.
Assert the count of first_sight whispers in whisperer.whispers is <= 3.
Publish 50 room_entered events for distinct (floor=1, room_id=0..49) on turn=2.
Assert CountingAdapter.call_count for that turn alone is <= 3 (the per-turn cap is respected for room_entered too).
criteria.threshold: Per-turn cap is honored for the new event types; the budget guardrail behaves as in Sprint 7.

criteria.description: Sprint 7 contract is fully preserved. All 22 Sprint-7 criteria continue to hold: event bus, EVENT_TYPES contains the original seven names (NOW PLUS the new ones), adapter ABC + Null + Offline + real-provider stubs, prose pool >= 8 entries per ORIGINAL event type, OfflineAdapter determinism, Whisperer auto-production, budget exhaustion fallback, adapter failure resilience, Game wiring of run_started + descended, no-network test posture, layering invariants, per-turn throttle.
testSteps: Run `pytest tests/test_sprint07.py` and verify ALL Sprint-7 tests pass unchanged.
Verify EVENT_TYPES is a SUPERSET of the original seven: {run_started, run_ended, entered_room, killed_monster, low_hp, found_item, descended} ⊆ EVENT_TYPES.
Verify the prose pool's ORIGINAL seven event-type entries have not lost any items (counts >= the Sprint-7-evaluated counts).
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer` and confirm exit 0 with the Sprint-2 grid still byte-deterministic across two runs.
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --whisperer null` and confirm no whisper text appears in stdout (NullAdapter contract preserved).
criteria.threshold: All 22 Sprint-7 tests pass; original event types and pool entries preserved; null/no-whisperer behavior unchanged.

criteria.description: Sprints 1-2 functionality is preserved: `python -m whisperdeep --seed N --headless --no-whisperer` prints the Sprint-2 dungeon frame deterministically; the documented glyphs (`#`, `.`, `+`, `<`, `>`, `@`) all appear in a multi-floor traversal; existing pytest tests from prior sprints all pass.
testSteps: Run `pytest tests/test_sprint01.py tests/test_generator.py tests/test_game.py` and verify the full prior test suite passes.
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer` twice; verify byte-identical stdout (Sprint-2 determinism).
Run with two different seeds and verify the grids differ.
Verify the canonical glyphs (`#`, `.`, `+`, `>`, `@`) appear in a `--seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer` frame; `<` is acceptable to be absent on floor 0 (pre-existing Sprint-1/2 behavior).
criteria.threshold: All prior sprint tests pass; Sprint-2 determinism + glyph contract preserved.

criteria.description: Automated test suite includes >= 12 NEW tests targeting Sprint 8 functionality. They cover at minimum: render_panel signature (C1), panel fixed-dimension wrapping (C2), sliding window of recent whispers (C3), composite render_frame_with_whispers (C4), CLI default panel + --no-whisperer + --no-panel (C5/C14), render_frame unchanged with whisperer on/off (C6), first_sight idempotent naming (C7), room_entered per-room dedupe (C8), pool extension (C9), name template substitution (C10), Game observe_kind hook (C12), end-to-end seed determinism (C13), edge-case panel rendering (C15), per-turn cap honored for new event types (C17). All tests pass with no API keys and no network.
testSteps: Locate test files under `tests/` whose names reference 'sprint08', 'panel', 'first_sight', 'room_entered', or 'naming'.
Count distinct `test_*` function definitions (not parameterized invocations) in those files; assert the count is >= 12.
Verify each scenario above is covered by at least one named test (grep test names + assertion content).
Run `pytest tests/` with no API keys set and assert all tests pass.
Verify no Sprint-8 test imports `requests`, `httpx`, `urllib.request`, `anthropic`, or `openai` at module top-level (extending the Sprint-7 no-network posture).
criteria.threshold: >= 12 distinct Sprint-8 test functions, all passing, covering each listed scenario; no network calls.

criteria.description: Documentation (README.md and/or docs/) describes Sprint 8: the whisper panel layout (right-of-grid or below-grid; documented choice), panel width/height defaults and CLI flags (--no-panel, --panel-width), the two new canonical event types (first_sight, room_entered) and when they fire, the per-category visual marker convention from C16, the first-sight name-template substitution convention from C10, and the per-(floor, room_id) dedupe rule from C8. The Sprint-7 docs are updated to reflect that whispers are NOW visible in-game (the 'plumbing-only' note from Sprint 7 is no longer accurate after Sprint 8).
testSteps: Open README.md and any sprint-relevant docs in `docs/`.
Verify a section describes the panel layout choice (right-of-grid vs below-grid) and width/height defaults.
Verify a section documents `--no-panel` and `--panel-width` (or the chosen names).
Verify a section enumerates the two new event types ('first_sight', 'room_entered'), with payload schemas.
Verify a section describes the per-category marker convention (room/sight/generic prefixes).
Verify a section describes the first_sight name template (placeholder syntax) and the per-(floor, room_id) dedupe rule.
Verify the prior 'whispers are NOT yet rendered' statement from Sprint 7 docs is updated/superseded.
criteria.threshold: All six documentation topics are present; Sprint-7 plumbing-only note is superseded.

criteria.description: Layering invariants from Sprint 7 are preserved AND the new Sprint-8 modules respect them. The render module imports from whisperdeep.whisperer ONLY for type hints (Whisper dataclass) — it does NOT import from whisperdeep.llm or whisperdeep.events directly. The Game module continues to depend only on the events bus and a factory; the panel renderer is wired in by the CLI / a renderer factory, not by Game. Concrete real-provider adapters are still constructed only by the adapter factory.
testSteps: Open whisperdeep/render.py (or whisperdeep/panel.py, if introduced) and verify (via grep or AST) it imports at most: stdlib, typing, the Whisper dataclass from whisperdeep.whisperer (or a Protocol/structural type), Game from whisperdeep.game (already true), Floor from whisperdeep.floor.
Verify the render/panel module does NOT import from whisperdeep.llm directly (it must not know about adapters).
Verify whisperdeep/game.py does NOT import the panel renderer (Game stays render-agnostic; the CLI calls the renderer).
Verify whisperdeep/events.py and whisperdeep/llm.py have NOT acquired imports from whisperdeep.render or whisperdeep.panel.
Verify EVENT_TYPES gained 'first_sight' and 'room_entered' WITHOUT removing or renaming any of the original seven names (additive change).
criteria.threshold: Layering invariants hold: render -> whisperer (Whisper type) + floor/game; panel never imports llm; game never imports panel; EVENT_TYPES change is additive.

outOfScope: Director-mode procgen nudges — the Whisperer must NOT mutate dungeon generation in this sprint (Sprint 9).
Cross-run meta-memory or persistence of minted names beyond the current run (Sprint 9/10).
Themed dungeon archetypes and color palette swaps for the panel (Sprint 11).
Curses / TUI / color codes / ANSI styling. The panel is plain ASCII (with optional box-drawing characters).
Rebindable keys, help overlay, ambient audio, leaderboard (Sprint 12).
Streaming LLM responses; the adapter contract remains single-shot prompt -> single-shot completion.
Caching previous LLM responses to disk or across runs.
Real network calls in CI / automated tests — provider adapters remain stubbed and gated.

Sprint-3/4/5/6 features (FOV, combat, items, inventory, full monster AI, run lifecycle / save-load). These remain unimplemented in tree; Sprint 8 publishes events only via the hooks already present (Game.from_seed spawn room, Game.descend) plus the new Game.observe_kind hook for first sight, and accepts that movement-driven room transitions may rely on a minimal room-detection helper added in this sprint rather than a full FOV system.
Inventory-driven first_sight: players cannot yet pick up items, so first-sight for items is exercised via Game.observe_kind, not a full pickup flow.
deliverables: A panel renderer (render_panel in whisperdeep/render.py OR a new whisperdeep/panel.py) producing a fixed-width/height ASCII block from a list of Whisper records, with documented wrapping and per-category markers.

A composite renderer (render_frame_with_whispers in whisperdeep/render.py OR a render_frame(game, with_panel=True) opt-in flag) that combines grid + panel without modifying the original render_frame output.
Two new canonical event types ('first_sight', 'room_entered') added to whisperdeep/events.EVENT_TYPES / EventType in a backward-compatible way.

Whisperer extensions: a per-run name registry for first_sight (idempotent on repeat kinds), a per-(floor, room_id) dedupe set for room_entered, and template substitution that replaces a documented placeholder in first_sight pool entries with the minted name.
Prose pool extension: >= 8 distinct entries each for 'first_sight' and 'room_entered', preserving all Sprint-7 entries.

Game integration: a Game.observe_kind(kind, category) hook that publishes first_sight (idempotent), and Game machinery that publishes room_entered for the spawn room, the descent landing room, and (where movement-driven detection is introduced) for new rooms entered by movement, all with per-(floor, room_id) dedupe.
CLI flags: --no-panel (suppress panel without disabling whisperer), --panel-width N. Both documented in --help. Default behavior shows the panel.
>= 12 NEW pytest tests under tests/ covering the scenarios listed in C20, all passing with no API keys and no network access.

README.md / docs updates covering: the panel layout choice, the new CLI flags, the two new event types and their payloads, the per-category marker convention, the first_sight name template, the per-(floor, room_id) dedupe rule, and a note that Sprint 7's 'plumbing-only' caveat is superseded by Sprint 8.
